registry.description: Contempt is the inner dismissal of another as beneath consideration — the attitude that treats someone as worthless, ridiculous, or unworthy of respect. The Hebrew vocabulary covers to lighten, to despise, to taunt, to reject as of no account. Contempt is one of the most socially toxic of inner states because it is incompatible with genuine relationship: the contemptuous person cannot love the one they despise. Jesus's warning in the Sermon on the Mount treats contempt (calling someone a fool) with the same gravity as murder, because both proceed from the same inner dismissal of the other's worth.
